description_long: This is a twin-engine, high-wing turboprop airplane with a predominantly white fuselage and red accents on the engines, wingtips, and tail fin. The aircraft features a straight, high-mounted wing configuration and a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage and is enclosed with multiple windows for visibility. The landing gear is of the tricycle type, with two main gear units under the wings and a nose gear beneath the cockpit. The propellers are mounted on the leading edge of each wing, and the aircraft displays registration markings on the fuselage and wings. The overall design suggests a regional or utility transport aircraft suitable for short to medium-haul flights.
description: A white, twin-engine high-wing turboprop airplane with red accents, a conventional tail, and tricycle landing gear. The cockpit is forward and enclosed, with visible propellers and registration markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the high-mounted straight wings, red engine nacelles, and the full length of the fuselage with the tail fin and registration visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the horizontal stabilizers, single vertical tail, and the symmetry of the wings and landing gear.
description_view_2: Angled side view displaying both engines, the cockpit windows, and the red tail fin with its distinctive logo.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the twin propellers, forward cockpit, and the width of the high-mounted wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all fixed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
